Features:
This BRD outlines the business objectives, stakeholders, functional and non-functional requirements, scope, constraints, and success criteria for React ChatBotify.
2. Business Objectives
2.1 Primary Objectives
Provide a reusable chatbot component for React applications.
Enable developers to easily configure chatbot conversations.
Support enterprise-level customization (themes, plugins, settings, styles).
Maintain backward compatibility across React versions (16–19).
Ensure strong extensibility via hooks and event-driven architecture.
2.2 Secondary Objectives
Enable analytics integration.
Support AI-driven chatbot logic.
Allow plugin ecosystem growth.
Support mobile-first responsive behavior.
Improve developer experience (DX).
3. Project Scope
3.1 In Scope
React chatbot component library
Conversation flow management
Settings and style customization
Event-driven architecture
Plugin framework
Theme support
Audio & voice services
Mobile support optimization
Unit, integration, and compatibility testing
CI/CD pipeline validation
3.2 Out of Scope
Hosting infrastructure
Backend AI implementation
Natural language processing engine
Enterprise SaaS hosting platform
Proprietary analytics backend
4. Stakeholders
5. Current State Analysis
React ChatBotify evolved from a simple chatbot library (v1) into a feature-rich v2 architecture with:
Modular folder structure
Separation of Settings and Styles
Custom Events system
Internal and External Hooks
Themes repository
Experimental Plugin architecture
Improved Mobile handling
Automated testing (Jest, Cypress, CI compatibility)
6. Functional Requirements
6.1 Chatbot Core
FR1: System must render a chatbot UI component.
FR2: System must allow configurable conversation flows.
FR3: System must support block-based conversation structure.
FR4: System must manage user inputs and bot responses.
FR5: System must support conversation paths logic.
6.2 Conversation Management
FR6: Developers must define conversation blocks via JSON/TS objects.
FR7: Each block must support attributes (messages, options, validation, etc.).
FR8: System must support conditional navigation between blocks.
FR9: System must support custom input validation logic.
6.3 Settings & Styles
FR10: System must accept a settings prop for functional configurations.
FR11: System must accept a styles prop for UI customization.
FR12: Default settings must be loaded if user does not supply configuration.
FR13: User settings must merge with default settings safely.
6.4 Events System
FR14: System must dispatch lifecycle events (e.g., message inject, input submit).
FR15: Developers must subscribe to events externally.
FR16: Events must allow integration with analytics, logging, or APIs.
Example:
RcbPostMessageInjectEvent
RcbUserSubmitInputEvent
6.5 Hooks System
FR17: System must expose external hooks for advanced control.
FR18: Internal hooks must remain encapsulated.
FR19: External hooks must act as filtering layer over internal logic.
6.6 Audio & Voice
FR20: System must support audio playback of messages.
FR21: System must support voice input via microphone.
FR22: Audio and voice must be toggleable via hooks.
6.7 Themes
FR23: System must support theme-based styling.
FR24: Themes must be externally maintainable.
FR25: Themes must integrate seamlessly with styles prop.
6.8 Plugins
FR26: System must support plugin injection.
FR27: Plugins must hook into events and hooks.
FR28: Plugins must remain loosely coupled.
6.9 Mobile Support
FR29: Chatbot must render full-screen mode on mobile.
FR30: System must handle mobile keyboard resizing behavior.
FR31: Resize listeners must adjust chatbot dimensions dynamically.
6.10 Testing
FR32: Unit testing via Jest.
FR33: Integration testing via Cypress.
FR34: Compatibility testing across React 16–19.
FR35: CI/CD must validate builds before release.
7. Non-Functional Requirements
7.1 Performance
NFR1: Avoid unnecessary re-renders using useMemo and useCallback.
NFR2: Optimize message rendering.
NFR3: Minimize bundle size.
7.2 Scalability
NFR4: Architecture must support plugin ecosystem growth.
NFR5: Conversation engine must scale to large flows.
7.3 Usability
NFR6: API must be intuitive.
NFR7: Documentation must clearly explain concepts (Conversations, Settings, Styles, Themes).
7.4 Maintainability
NFR8: Clear folder structure separation.
NFR9: Code documentation required for major components.
NFR10: TODO comments must track technical debt.
7.5 Compatibility
NFR11: Must support React 16, 17, 18, 19.
NFR12: Must work across modern browsers.
NFR13: Mobile browser compatibility (iOS Safari, Android Chrome).
Modules:
8. Architecture Overview
8.1 High-Level Architecture Layers
UI Layer (Components)
State Layer (Contexts)
Logic Layer (Hooks & Services)
Configuration Layer (Settings & Styles)
Integration Layer (Events & Plugins)
9. Assumptions
Developers have basic React knowledge.
Applications are client-side React-based.
Consumers understand JSON/TypeScript configuration.
Browser APIs are available for voice/audio features.

Issues:
10. Constraints
Dependent on React ecosystem.
Browser security constraints for microphone/audio.
Backward compatibility requirements.
Plugin documentation not yet finalized.
11. Risks & Mitigation
12. Success Criteria
Library adoption growth.
Stable releases without regressions.
Plugin ecosystem expansion.
Reduced GitHub issue frequency.
Improved developer onboarding experience.
13. Future Roadmap (Indicative)
AI integration templates
Plugin marketplace
Visual flow builder
Enhanced analytics support
Accessibility compliance improvements
14. Approval
This BRD defines the functional and strategic direction for React ChatBotify and serves as a baseline for architectural discussions, sprint planning, and further enhancements.
